content_id:
[Kembali ke daftar isi] [98] Bukhari, Muslim, dan Ibn Abi Syaibah (12/110/2). Disebutkan dalam Irwa (no. 8) [99] Abu Dawud dan Hakim, yang menyatakannya sahih dan Dhahabi menyetujui. [100] Demikian dalam sebagian besar riwayat; dalam beberapa, tersebut wa ana min al-muslimin ("aku adalah salah satu orang Muslim"). Kemungkinan besar hal ini karena kesalahan salah seorang perawi, dan bukti lain menunjukkan ke arah itu, sehingga orang yang shalat seharusnya mengucapkan: wa ana awwal al-muslimin ("aku adalah yang pertama dari orang-orang Muslim"). Tidak ada yang salah dengan itu, bertentangan dengan apa yang dikatakan oleh sebagian orang dengan anggapan bahwa ini berarti "Aku adalah orang pertama yang memiliki sifat ini, sementara orang lain tidak." Tetapi tidak demikian; frasa ini sebenarnya merepresentasikan berlomba untuk memenuhi perintah — serupa dengan ini adalah "Katakanlah: jika Allah Yang Maha Pengasih memiliki seorang anak, maka aku adalah yang pertama dari para penyembah" (az-Zukhruf 43:81) dan ucapan Musa alaihis salam, "dan aku adalah yang pertama dari orang-orang beriman." (al-Araf 7:143) [101] Azhari berkata: yaitu "Aku tidak menyembah selain Engkau." [102] labbaik: aku hadir dengan teguh dan terus-menerus dalam ketaatan kepada-Mu; sadaik: sangat bahagia di bawah perintah-Mu dan taat mengikuti agama yang telah Engkau pilih. [103] Yaitu kejahatan tidak dapat dikaitkan kepada Allah karena tidak ada yang buruk dalam tindakan-Nya, karena semuanya baik, mulai dari keadilan hingga anugerah hingga kebijaksanaan, yang semuanya baik tanpa ada yang buruk di dalamnya. Tetapi kejahatan itu buruk karena tidak dapat dikaitkan kepada Allah. Ibn al-Qayyim rahimahullah berkata: "Dia adalah Pencipta kebaikan dan kejahatan, tetapi kejahatan ada pada sebagian makhluk-Nya, bukan dalam tindakan penciptaan-Nya maupun dalam tindakan-Nya. Oleh karena itu Yang Mahatinggi terbebas dari segala zulm, yang pada dasarnya adalah meletakkan sesuatu bukan pada tempatnya yang sebenarnya. Dia tidak meletakkan sesuatu kecuali di tempat yang sesuai, sehingga semuanya baik. Tetapi kejahatan adalah meletakkan sesuatu bukan pada tempatnya yang sebenarnya: ketika diletakkan pada tempatnya yang sebenarnya itu bukan kejahatan, maka pastikanlah bahwa kejahatan bukan dari-Nya. ...Tetapi jika dikatakan: Mengapa Dia menciptakan sesuatu yang jahat? Saya akan berkata: Dia yang menciptakan, dan tindakan-Nya adalah baik bukan jahat, karena penciptaan dan tindakan ada pada Allah, dan tidak mungkin kejahatan ada pada, atau dikaitkan kepada, Allah. Apa pun yang jahat dalam makhluk tidak dapat dikaitkan kepada Allah, tetapi tindakan-Nya dan ciptaan-Nya dapat dikaitkan kepada-Nya, sehingga semuanya baik." Sisa pembahasan penting ini beserta kesimpulannya dapat ditemukan dalam bukunya Syifa al-Alil fi Masail al-Qada wal-Qadar wat-Talil (hal. 178-206). [104] Muslim, Abu Awanah, Abu Dawud, Nasai, Ibn Hibban, Ahmad, Syafii, dan Tabarani; mereka yang mengkhususkannya untuk shalat sunat adalah keliru. [105] Nasai dengan isnad yang sahih. [106] Nasai dan Daraqutni dengan isnad yang sahih. [107] Yaitu aku memuliakan Engkau, artinya aku menganggap Engkau benar-benar bebas dari segala kekurangan. [108] Yaitu kami terbenam dalam pujian kepada-Mu. [109] Yaitu berkah nama-Mu sangat besar, karena kebaikan yang besar bermunculan dari mengingat nama-Mu. [110] Yaitu Kemuliaan dan Keagungan-Mu. [111] Abu Dawud dan Hakim, yang menyatakannya sahih dan Dhahabi menyetujui. Uqaili berkata (hal. 103): "ini telah diriwayatkan melalui beberapa jalur dengan isnad yang baik." Disebutkan dalam Irwa (no. 341). Diriwayatkan oleh Ibn Mandah dalam at-Tawhid (123/2) dengan isnad yang sahih dan Nasai dalam al-Yaum wal-Laylah sebagai mauquf dan marfu, sebagaimana dalam Jami al-Masanid oleh Ibn Kasir (jil. 3, bagian 2, hal. 235/2). [112] Abu Dawud dan Tahawi dengan isnad yang hasan.

[113] Muslim dan Abu Awanah; dinyatakan sahih oleh Tirmizi. Abu Nuaim juga meriwayatkannya dalam Akhbar Isfahan (1/210) dari Jubair ibn Mutim yang mendengar Nabi shallallahu alaihi wa sallam mengucapkannya dalam shalat sunat. [114] Muslim dan Abu Awanah. [115] Yaitu Engkau adalah Pemberi Cahaya bagi mereka, dan mereka yang ada di dalamnya diberi petunjuk oleh-Mu. [116] Yaitu Pelindung dan Pengawas yang terus-menerus terhadap mereka. [117] haqq: kebenaran, realitas. [118] Bukhari, Muslim, Abu Awanah, Abu Dawud, Ibn Nasr, dan Darimi. [119] Meskipun itu jelas tidak mengecualikan penggunaannya dalam shalat wajib juga, kecuali bagi imam, agar tidak memperpanjang shalat bagi makmum. [120] Muslim dan Abu Awanah. [121] Ahmad, Ibn Abi Syaibah (12/119/2), Abu Dawud, dan Tabarani dalam Mujam al-Ausat (62/2) dengan satu isnad sahih, dan isnad lainnya hasan. [122] Tayalisi dan Abu Dawud dengan isnad yang sahih.
content_en:
[Back to contents] [98] Bukhaari , Muslim & Ibn Abi Shaibah (12/110/2). It is given in Irwaa' (no. 8) [99] Abu Daawood & Haakim, who declared it sahih and Dhahabi agreed. [100] It is thus in most of the narrations; in some, it is wa ana min al-muslimeen ("I am one of the Muslims"). It is likely that this is because of the mistake of one of the narrators, and other evidence points to that, so the worshipper should say: wa ana min al-muslimeen ("I am the first of the Muslims"). There is nothing wrong with that, contrary to what some people say under the impression that this means "I am the first person who has this quality, while the rest of the people do not." But it is not so; this phrase actually represents competing to fulfil orders - similar to this is "Say: if the Merciful God has a son, then I am the first of the worshippers" (Zukhruf 43:81) and the saying of Moosa (`alaihis salam), "and I am the first of the believers." (A`raaf 7:143) [101] Azhari said: i.e. `I do not worship anything other than You.' [102] labbaik: I am firmly & continually present in Your obedience; sa`daik: extremely happy under Your order and devoutly following the deen which You have chosen. [103] i.e. Evil cannot be traced back to Allaah because there is nothing bad in His actions, for they are all good, ranging from justice to grace to wisdom, all of which are good with no bad in them. But evil is evil because it cannot be traced back to Allaah. Ibn al-Qayyim (rahimahullaah) said: "He is the Creator of good and evil, but the evil exists in some of His creatures, not in His act of creating nor in His actions. Hence the Exalted is cleared of any zulm, which is fundamentally to put something in other than its proper place. He does not put anything except in its suitable place, so that is all good. But evil is to put something in other than its proper place: when it is put in its proper place it is not evil, so be sure that evil is not from Him. ...But if it is said: Why did He create something which is evil? I would say: He did the creating, and His action is good not evil, for creation and action is with Allaah, and it is impossible for evil to be with, or attributed to, Allaah. Anything evil in the created cannot be traced back to Allaah, but His actions and His creation can be attributed to Him, so they are good." The rest of this important discussion as well as its conclusion is to be found in his book Shifaa' al-`Aleel fi Masaa'il al-Qadaa' wal-Qadr wat-Ta`leel (pp. 178-206). [104] Muslim, Abu `Awaanah, Abu Daawood, Nasaa'i Ibn Hibbaan, Ahmad, Shaafi'i & Tabaraani; those who specify it to optional prayers are mistaken. [105] Nasaa'i with a sahih isnaad. [106] Nasaa'i & Daaraqutni with a sahih isnaad. [107] i.e I glorify You, meaning I consider You totally free from any deficiency. [108] i.e. we are submerged in Your praise. [109] i.e. the blessings of Your Name are great, for great good springs from the remembrance of Your Name. [110] i.e. Your Glory and Might. [111] Abu Daawood & Haakim, who declared it sahih and Dhahabi agreed. `Uqaili said (p. 103): "this has been narrated via several routes with good isnaads." It is given in 'Irwaa (no. 341) Transmitted by Ibn Mandah in At-tawhid (123/2) with a sahih isnaad & Nasaa'i in al-Yawm wal-Laylah as mawquf and marfoo`, as in Jaami` al-Masaaneed of Ibn Katheer (vol. 3 part 2 p. 235/2) [112] Abu Daawood & Tahaawi with a hasan isnaad.

[113] Muslim & Abu `Awaanah; declared sahih by Tirmidhi. Abu Nu`aim also narrated it in Akhbaar Isbahaan (1/210) from Jubair ibn Mut`am who heard the Prophet (sallallaahu `alaihi wa sallam) saying it in voluntary prayer. [114] Muslim & Abu `Awaanah. [115] i.e. You are the Giver of Light to them, and those in them are guided by You. [116] i.e. the Protector and the constant Watcher over them. [117] haqq: truth, reality. [118] Bukhaari , Muslim, Abu `Awaanah, Abu Daawood, Ibn Nasr & Daarimi. [119] Although that clearly does not rule out using them in the obligatory prayers also, except for the imaam, so that he does not prolong the prayer for the followers. [120] Muslim & Abu `Awaanah. [121] Ahmad, Ibn Abi Shaibah (12/119/2), Abu Daawood & Tabaraani in Mu`jam al-Awsat (62/2) with one isnaad sahih, and another hasan. [122] Tayaalisi & Abu Daawood with a sahih isnaad.
content_ar: [Back to contents] [98] Bukhaari , مسلم & Ibn Abi Shaibah (12/110/2). It is given in Irwaa' (no. 8) [99] Abu Daawood & Haakim, who declared it صحيح and Dhahabi agreed. [100] It is وهكذا in most of the narrations; in some, it is wa ana min al-muslimeen ("I am one of the المسلمون"). It is likely that this is لأن of the mistake of one of the narrators, and other evidence points to that, لذا the worshipper should say: wa ana min al-muslimeen ("I am the أولاً of the المسلمون"). There is nothing الخطأ with that, contrary to what some people say under the impression that this means "I am the أولاً person who has this quality, بينما the rest of the people do not." But it is not لذا; this phrase actually represents competing to fulfil orders - similar to this is "Say: إذا the Merciful God has a son, then I am the أولاً of the worshippers" (Zukhruf 43:81) and the saying of Moosa (`alaihis salam), "and I am the أولاً of the المؤمنون." (A`raaf 7:143) [101] Azhari said: i.e. `I do not worship anything other than You.' [102] labbaik: I am firmly & continually present in Your obedience; sa`daik: extremely happy under Your order and devoutly following the deen which You have chosen. [103] i.e. الشر cannot be traced back to Allaah لأن there is nothing bad in His actions, for they are all الخير, ranging from العدل to الفضل to الحكمة, all of which are الخير with no bad in them. But الشر is الشر لأن it cannot be traced back to Allaah. ابن القيم (rahimahullaah) said: "He is the Creator of الخير and الشر, but the الشر exists in some of His creatures, not in His act of creating nor in His actions. ومن ثَم the Exalted is cleared of any zulm, which is بشكل أساسي to put something in other than its proper place. He does not put anything except in its suitable place, لذا that is all الخير. But الشر is to put something in other than its proper place: عندما it is put in its proper place it is not الشر, لذا be sure that الشر is not from Him. ...But إذا قيل: Why did He create something which is الشر? I would say: He did the creating, and His action is الخير not الشر, for creation and action is with Allaah, and it is impossible for الشر to be with, or attributed to, Allaah. Anything الشر in the created cannot be traced back to Allaah, but His actions and His creation can be attributed to Him, لذا they are الخير." The rest of this المهم discussion as well as its conclusion is to be found in his الكتاب Shifaa' al-`Aleel fi Masaa'il al-Qadaa' wal-Qadr wat-Ta`leel (pp. 178-206). [104] مسلم, Abu `Awaanah, Abu Daawood, Nasaa'i Ibn Hibbaan, أحمد, Shaafi'i & Tabaraani; those who specify it to optional الصلوات are mistaken. [105] Nasaa'i with a صحيح isnaad. [106] Nasaa'i & Daaraqutni with a صحيح isnaad. [107] i.e I glorify You, meaning I consider You totally free from any deficiency. [108] i.e. we are submerged in Your praise. [109] i.e. the البركات of Your Name are great, for great الخير springs from the الذكر of Your Name. [110] i.e. Your Glory and Might.
